reasoning: The Adjusted National Financial Conditions Index (ANFCI) recently reached a very low, highly accommodative level (-0.588 on Jan 16) but has begun to tick upward, reaching -0.581 on Jan 30. Recent news from late January 2026 indicates a worsening of financial conditions: an AI/speculative index fell 20% from its highs, consumer confidence plunged to a 12-year low, and markets experienced a sell-off driven by tariff threats from the newly inaugurated President Trump and the pricing out of Fed rate cuts (which flattens the yield curve). Because the ANFCI increases when financial conditions tighten, these events strongly suggest the index will continue its recent upward trajectory in the near term. Since -0.581 is already an extremely low value dynamically, mean reversion and the asymmetric risk of volatility spikes provide additional upward pressure. For the nearer date (Feb 8), the momentum is clear and expectations of tighter conditions are high (75%). For the later date (Mar 3), there is greater uncertainty as markets could digest the tariff shock or rebound (65%).
